--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -130,2 +130,19 @@
   - No social post; let operational changes prove themselves.
 - Watch next week: A $69 conversion, support backlog age, compute spend under higher quotas, tier 2 capacity headroom, and whether renewals begin lifting cash.
+
+## Week 9 / Day 56
+
+- Cash: $363,834. Subscribers: 6,501. Week 8 added 2,759 subscribers from 4,566 leads; cash increased by ~$49k despite higher spend.
+- New S2 conversion remained strong at A $69: content S2 produced 4,217 leads from $35,000 and 2,542 paid S2 content subscribers. Subscription payments were $173,364.
+- Costs: ops -$64,400, ads -$35,000, compute -$9,456, dev -$9,100, capacity -$3,710. Growth is now cash-positive at current scale.
+- Active mix: S2 A 6,338, S2 B 89, S2 C 1. A remains the core product.
+- Issues: 507 open; S2 has 502 all fresh, but S1 has a persistent 17-day issue and several older S1 issues. Cancellations are still low but rising.
+- Capacity: tier 2 showed 4.4% peak overload, so moved to tier 3.
+- Changes:
+  - Capacity raised to tier 3 (2.5M units/day).
+  - New prices: A $79, B $199, C $349. S2 first month with promo is $69.
+  - Quotas raised to A 3000, B 12000, C 30000.
+  - Dev remains maintenance: $1,300/day total.
+  - Ops increased to $13,800/day total: global $4,000, S1 $1,500, S2 $8,000, S3 $300.
+  - Ads scaled to S2 content $8,000/day.
+- Watch next week: A $79 conversion, tier 3 capacity headroom, compute cost under higher quotas, issue age, churn rate, and renewal cash.
